title: agropyron cristatum subsp. bulbosum
intro: Agropyron cristatum subsp. bulbosum, also known as bulbous wheatgrass, is a subspecies of Agropyron cristatum, a type of grass native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of phenolic compounds and other bioactive substances. The health benefits of Agropyron cristatum subsp. bulbosum include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as possessing antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The plant can be prepared as herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in the production of dietary supplements and other health-promoting products.
